title: Stenophragma pumilum
intro: Stenophragma pumilum, also known as Dwarf Stenophragma, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stenophragma pumilum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stenophragma pumilum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Stenophragma pumilum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the genus Stenophragma. The family Gesneriaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet or Gesneriad family. Stenophragma pumilum is native to Madagascar, where it grows as an epiphyte in humid environments. The plant is often found in cloud forests and subtropical regions.
